ownership_note: Try phone photos of your real routine first ,  shoes by the actual shoes, toothbrush by the sink. Buy cards only if DIY visuals are not sticking after two weeks.
safety_note_public: Small cards and tokens need supervision around younger siblings.
educational_objective: Pictures can help when words are not enough: shoes, teeth, toilet, coat, bag. The aim is not a perfect routine; it is giving your child something concrete to follow.
age_stage_nuance: Versus 31, 33m: jobs feel real ,  choose clothes, carry the bag, brush teeth without you narrating every step. Photos of your actual routine may beat generic cards.
what_to_look_for: Photo-first or clear home symbols; short strips for door or Now/Next; wipe-clean durable cards; Ages 2+ parseable marks; buyable UK URLs; parent-buy utility not gift packaging.
what_to_avoid: Under-36 / bare Ages 3+ listings for Top Picks in this band; Etsy/Temu printables; whole-day charts when one transition hurts; timers as first buy before 36m.
methodology: Benchmarked UK visual-routine cards and boards (Create Visual Aids, Craftly, Little Goose) against the nearly-three independence brief: shoes, teeth, coat, bag without narrating every step. Screened out timers and ASD boards carrying under-36 or Ages 3+ marks incompatible with 34, 36m. Captured structured age_signals as Ages 2+ parent-setup tools from brand copy and preschool-parent reviews. Ranked with buying_factor_memo (photos-first, age overlap, job-fit, buyability, evidence). URL-smoked and availability-checked every Top 5 primary on 2026-07-22 via stage3-url-preflight.mjs and stage3-availability-check.mjs before locking order. Updated 2026-07-23: parent copy rewritten to web/docs/brand/WRITING_GUIDELINES.md; Craftly Top Picks with rating_count < 15 replaced by Haliburtons / Amazon morning / Now Next Later.
buying_factor_memo: Five factors set order for 34, 36m visual routines. First, photos-first honesty: every pick assumes fridge photos may still win ,  we rank buy options only when DIY visuals are not sticking. Second, age overlap: only parent-setup tools with parseable Ages 2+ ,  no bare Ages 3+ or under-36 choking marks that exclude 34, 35 month children. Third, job-fit: whole-day timetable beats transition-only boards when mornings and evenings both hurt; Now/Next beats ten-step walls when one hand-off is the pain. Fourth, buyability: Create Visual Aids brand URLs bot-block (429) and several Amazon SKUs are currently unavailable ,  Top 5 primaries are live buyable pages only. Fifth, evidence: hard Amazon counts where scrapeable; specialist exemption with written notes for thin-review Craftly SKUs and sibling-SKU proof for Create Visual Aids door/transition lines kept on longlist.

guidance_notes.text: Try photos of your real routine on the fridge or cupboard first — shoes by the actual shoes, toothbrush by the sink. Buy cards only if DIY visuals are not sticking after two weeks.
For this band, skip Top Picks labelled Ages 3+ or not suitable under 36 months — that excludes 34–35 month children. Prefer Ages 2+ parent-setup tools.
If you used a simpler picture strip earlier, bring it back out with fewer steps and let your nearly-three reorder the cards — the shift is real jobs, not new kit.
Hold dedicated visual timers until 36m+ if listings carry under-36 choking marks. A phone timer on the bathroom shelf is enough for teeth jobs now.
Hold off on full-day timetables if only one transition hurts — a Now/Next board or door strip may be enough.

qa_summary.notes: Picks 1 and 4 meet numeric rating bar. Picks 2, 3, and 5 use specialist exemption. Create Visual Aids door/Now-Next SKUs on longlist due to buyability. ASD timers skipped for age gate.

ingestion_ready.founder_review_notes: Full Mode A re-research 2026-07-22. Top 5 primaries are buyable smoke-checked URLs. Photos-first doctrine. Three Craftly picks + two Create Visual Aids via Amazon. Out The Door on longlist until Amazon restocks.
ingestion_ready.notes: Parent copy upgraded to Writing Guidelines v1 (2026-07-23). Re-run FF before ingest if URLs changed.
